_instructions: Edit the values below with your real content. Do not edit the field names. Add or remove objects inside 'sections' to add or remove sections from the site - the site will render however many you include (up to 10 is the tested range). Put your real images in an /images folder and reference them by filename in 'image'. 'site.cta' is optional - omit it entirely to hide the hero button.

site.title: India Safety Dashboard
site.tagline: A data-driven look at governance failure patterns behind fatal public safety incidents in urban India, 2016-2026, based on open source data.
site.heroImageAlt: Aerial view of a lakeside Indian town at golden hour, with dense waterside buildings reflected in the water.

sections.heading: Governance Failure, Mapped
sections.body: An interactive map plots every documented incident across urban India by city and state, sized by death toll and colored by category - click any point to read the full record.
sections.imageAlt: Aerial view of a lakeside Indian town at golden hour, buildings reflected in the water.
sections.caption: Interactive incident map

sections.heading: Where the System Breaks Down
sections.body: Each incident is traced through the regulatory chain that should have prevented it - licence, inspection, enforcement, response, and conviction - to see exactly where accountability stalls.
sections.imageAlt: Dense hillside town in India blanketed in fog, with colorful rooftops climbing the slope.
sections.caption: Licence to inspection to enforcement to response to conviction

sections.heading: Known Risks, Documented Warnings
sections.body: Many incidents had a documented prior warning - a notice issued, a building flagged, an approval denied - before anyone died. The dashboard surfaces how often a known risk preceded a preventable failure.
sections.imageAlt: Busy Indian street market with cycle rickshaws, shop signage, and pedestrians.
sections.caption: Enforcement failure analysis

sections.heading: Built for Reform
sections.body: Compiled from news reports, government inquiries, court records, CAG audits, and credible media investigations - built for city administrators, regulators, and policymakers who need evidence, not anecdotes.
sections.imageAlt: Quiet narrow residential alley between aging apartment buildings in an Indian city.
sections.caption: Open data, by Janaagraha

footer.credit: India Safety Dashboard - an open governance-data project by Janaagraha
